page: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the LASv3 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This configuration features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 16015 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance is also strong in stress-ng, though Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks fall into the middle 50% average tier. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier and small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 25%, while large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier.

Given the high local storage capacity of over 16 TB and the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a resource-dense profile suited for data-intensive applications. It demonstrates strong performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, making it suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing. The lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it maintains average performance for CPU-based LLM inference. The high density of local storage and memory provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive local scratch space or large in-memory datasets without relying on external storage attachments.
description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is an x86_64 server in the LASv3 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 16015 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU scores in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly competitive, highlighted by a top 10% Passmark memory score. The server excels in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, both ranking in the top 25%, while maintaining average performance in single-core tasks and LLM inference. This resource-dense configuration is optimized for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads, such as large databases, data warehousing, caching layers, and software compilation, offering high qualitative cost efficiency by combining massive local storage with substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB of local storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, it delivers strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB of local storage, optimized for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and over sixteen terabytes of local storage.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
512 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core resource ratio
16015 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage for data-intensive applications
Strong multi-core CPU and database performance placing in the top 25% of benchmarks
Top 10% tier Passmark memory score indicating high-speed memory subsystem performance
Suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing
